previous_headings: II. Input specifications
title: Specifications
text: functions metrics S4 methods implemented multiple primary data input classes. primary input (following R tradition) parametrized x. x parameter can following can following input data structures: coordmat: two-column tabular structure inherits matrix. simplest input format, implies long-lat two columns. coorddat: data.frame geographic coordinates. column coordinates need specified, default long="long" lat="lat". sfpoints: multipoint object, either classes sf sfc. sfpolygons: multipolygon class object sf sfc. icell: Vector cell identifiers icosahedral grid (icosa::hexagrid icosa::trigrid), character vector. Note can used together igrid type icosahedral grid. rast: SpatRaster class object defined terra package. case input types, specific components can specified following parameters. - long: Column name indicating longitudes (character) - lat: Column name indicating latitudes (character) - tax: Column name indicating taxa, functional units can used split dataset.

text: list additional parameters may necessary metrics. s: pre-specified structure. sf: ‘simple feature’ type object, usually polygon multipolygon style object class sf sfc p: Geographic coordinates (long, lat) single point given two-column matrix. icosa: icosahedral grid object defined either icosa::trigrid derived class icosa::hexagrid. mask: Another spatial structure allows exclusion certain area calculation metric. q: single real number range [0,1], expressing confidence critical interval, numeric. dm: distance matrix may replace default distance matrix (e.g. great circle distance matrix). Frequently used refine spatial environment, e.g. consider distances water, land, etc. unique: logical switch indicating whether duplicate coordinates omitted calculations, can effects results cases (e.g. centroid calculation). sake efficiency transparency, set TRUE default, means every coordinate pair considered calculations. effects can explored deliberate inclusion duplicates.

title: Specifications

title: Specifications
text: [x] | mgcd | cells, grid, q | MGCD cell centroids, great circle distance quantile. | [x] | mgcd | coords, q | maximum great circle distance points | [x] | cenrad | coords, q | Centroid radius points, maximum quantile distance. | [x] | cenrad | cells, q | Centroid radius points, maximum quantile distance. | [x] | latrange | coords | latitudinal range point cloud. | [ ] | zoneaarea | coords | spherical surface area zone defined latitudinal range point cloud. | [ ] | zoneaarea | coords/cells, grid | latitudinal range point cloud. | [x] | mstlength | coords | length minimum spanning tree based set points | [ ] | mstlength | cells, grid | length minimum spanning tree based centers cells. | [ ] | chull_cell | coords, grid | number cells convex hull occupies. | [ ] | chull_cell | cells, grid | number cells convex hull occupies. | [ ] | ahull_cell | cells, grid | number cells covered alpha hull surface sphere. | [ ] | ahull_cell | coords, grid | number cells covered alpha hull surface sphere. | [ ] | mincircle | coords, q | minimum small circle area, radius position covers q proportion points | [ ] | minellipse | coords, q | covering spherical ellipse set points, shorter longer axis . | [ ] | density | coords, q | densitiy occurrence records |

what: Spherical Ranges and Parametrization of Geographic Shapes
title: Spherical Ranges and Parametrization of Geographic Shapes
text: package collation tools metrics characterize distribution data surfrace sphere ellipsoid. primary group metrics describe extent distribution, .e. geographic ranges. calculation geographic ranges can executed using point coordinates data, vector polygons, well cells discretized sphere. Besides ensuring use geometrically correct implementations, package offers exploration partial results visual diagnostics.

title: Spherical Ranges and Parametrization of Geographic Shapes
text: functions package dependent geographic discretization structures, dependencies package - primaril vector-spatial objects icosahedral grids, implemented sf icosa packages, respectively.

title: Spherical Ranges and Parametrization of Geographic Shapes
code: library(orange)  # background map library(chronosphere) ne <- fetch("NaturalEarth", verbose=FALSE)  # some example raw data (Pinna nobilis from OBIS) data(pinna) # only coords coords <- unique(pinna[, c("decimallongitude", "decimallatitude")])  # rename to standard colnames(coords) <- c("long", "lat")  # omit anomaly  coords <- coords[-855, ]   # basic plot plot(ne$geometry, reset=FALSE, xlim=c(-15, 40), ylim=c(30, 50),     col="#ffe7ba", border=NA) points(coords, pch=3, col="#5d7327", cex=2)  # Maximum great circle distance mgcd <- mgcd(coords, plot=TRUE)  # Occupancy on a hexagonal grid hex <- hexagrid(deg=2, sf=TRUE) # from package icosa occ <- occupancy(coords, s=hex, plot=TRUE)  # Minimum spanning tree length mst <- mstlength(coords, plot=TRUE, plot.args=list(lwd=3)) # Maximum great circle distance mgcd ## [1] 3452.84 # Occupancy count  occ ## [1] 23

title: Samples generated from various Kent distributions — kentsamples
text: liest 16 matrices (1000 x 2) columns equal longitudes latitudes. scientificname Taxon name. central_s Central position, small spread. central_m Central position, medium spread. central_l Central position, large spread. central_xl Central position, extra large spread. arctic_s Arctic position, small spread. arctic_m Arctic position, medium spread. arctic_l Arctic position, large spread. arctic_xl Arctic position, extra large spread. antarctic_s Antarctic position, small spread. antarctic_m Antarctic position, medium spread. antarctic_l Antarctic position, large spread. antarctic_xl Antarctic position, extra large spread. dateline_s Dateline position, small spread. dateline_m Dateline position, medium spread. dateline_l Dateline position, large spread. dateline_xl Dateline position, extra large spread.

title: Samples generated from various Kent distributions — kentsamples
text: set coordinates generated Kent distributions sample number n = 1000. samples elements list, 16 total combination location spread (.e. kappa parameter Kent distribution). four locations: central (0°N 0°E), arctic (85°N 5°E), antarctic (85°S 5°E) dateline (170°E 5°N), four sizes: small (s, kappa = 50), medium (m, kappa = 15), large (l, kappa = 5), extra-large (xl, kappa = 2).

code: # generate 3 points on a sphere ps <- icosa::rpsphere(3, output="polar") small <- sc_center(x=ps) circle<- sc_points(x=small$center, r=small$r, output="polar") #> Error in sc_points(x = small$center, r = small$r, output = "polar"): could not find function "sc_points"  plot(NULL, NULL, xlim=c(-180, 180), ylim=c(-90, 90)) points(ps, col="red", pch=3, cex=4) points(small$center, col="blue", pch=16)  points(circle, col="green", pch=16) #> Error: object 'circle' not found

title: orange 0.1.0-7 - 2026-04-30
text: kentsamples data testing demonstration. sc_shape function calculate points small circles sc_center function calculate small circle based points sphere sc_in function calculate whether bunch points inside small circle (based planes, rather distances, 10x faster).

title: orange 0.1.0-7 - 2026-04-30
text: Omitted likely irrelevant functions (based initial screening). Omitted ranges_ prefix range calculation functions. function cenrad renamed radius Refactored functions standardized interface.
